MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: DC growth held 93% FY26 full-year; Q1 FY27 guide $43B reinforces Blackwell Ultra ramp trajectory; China optionality fully impaired but demand supply-constrained not demand-constrained.

shifts.trend: DC revenue: Q3 FY26 $30.8B (+112% YoY run-rate) → FY26 full year $115.2B (+93%) → Q1 FY27 guided ~$39B DC implied
shifts.note: Growth rate normalizing from triple-digits to still very high 60-70% YoY as base effect grows

shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 customers each >10% (22/15/13/11%); FY26 10-K maintained same 4-way concentration
shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependency structurally elevated; capex cycle risk if one player pauses

shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0B vs $8.1B Q3 prior year; H20 $4.5B Q1 FY26 charge; H200 Feb 2026 license carries 25% tariff
shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired — model at zero for scenario planning

shifts.trend: Q4 FY26 guide $37.5B → FY26 10-K guided Q1 FY27 $43.0B (+15% QoQ); each guide met or exceeded
shifts.note: Supply-constrained not demand-constrained; data center energy/capital availability remains gating factor

NVDA.verdict: Thesis fully intact. Q1 FY27 guide of $43B is the clearest data point: Blackwell Ultra ramp is real. China is zero and should stay zero in models. Customer concentration is a monitoring risk but hyperscalers can't pause AI capex without ceding AI lead.

CSCO.headline: Three-quarter streak of Networking re-acceleration on AI Infrastructure; FY26 guidance raised twice as Splunk integration delivers and AI campus spend converts.

shifts.trend: Networking revenue YoY: +18% (Q1) → +21% (Q2) → +20% (Q3); FY26 guidance raised Q1→Q3 from $59.0B to $60.5-61.5B
shifts.note: AI Infrastructure (800G spine, Silicon One) winning hyperscaler and enterprise AI campus build-outs

shifts.trend: Security revenue recovering: on-prem license decline masking Splunk cloud subscription ramp; Splunk ARR $3.8B (Q2) → >$4B (Q3)
shifts.note: Recurring revenue quality improving despite reported security segment headwind

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP gross margin 65.5-65.8% across three quarters; operating leverage on Splunk integration synergies
shifts.note: Margin mix stable with software/subscription growth offsetting hardware pricing pressure

shifts.trend: FY26 guide: $59.0B initial → $59.6-60.6B (Q2 raise) → $60.5-61.5B (Q3 raise); Q3 EPS guide $1.00-1.02
shifts.note: Consistent beat-and-raise cadence as AI Networking converts faster than expected

CSCO.verdict: AI Networking tailwind is real and compounding — each quarter adds a new layer of committed hyperscaler and enterprise AI campus orders. Splunk ARR building creates durable recurring revenue. The investment case is strengthening, not just holding.

AMD.headline: Data Center growth re-accelerating to +57% in Q1 FY26 from +32% in FY25; OpenAI 6GW deal converts from announcement to active ramp; gross margin expanding 3pts on MI350/EPYC mix.

shifts.trend: Data Center: FY25 $16.6B (+32%) → Q1 FY26 $5.8B (+57% YoY); Q2 FY26 guidance $11.25B midpoint total implies DC ~$6.5B+
shifts.note: MI350 GPU and 5th-gen EPYC Turin driving hyperscaler server refresh wins

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP gross margin: 50% (FY25) → 53% (Q1 FY26) → ~54% Q2 guide
shifts.note: Data Center mix enrichment compounding; MI325 China license tariff (25%) manageable drag

shifts.trend: MI308 $800M charge (Q2 FY25) reversed $360M (Q4 FY25); MI325 partial licenses Feb 2026 with 25% US re-import tariff
shifts.note: China optionality partially restored but tariff-burdened — not zero, but not zero value

shifts.trend: Q1 FY26 guide $10.0-10.5B → printed $10.3B; Q2 guide $11.0-11.5B with DC outpacing blended growth
shifts.note: Consistent beat on Data Center; OpenAI 6GW deal adds multi-year structural demand floor

AMD.verdict: AMD's DC re-acceleration to +57% confirms EPYC market share gains and Instinct GPU ramp are real. The OpenAI 6GW deal converts from optionality to contracted revenue. China risk is now manageable with partial licenses. The investing case has materially improved since the FY25 10-K.

ANET.headline: Revenue growth re-accelerating from +19% FY25 to +35% Q1 FY26 as Cloud Titan AI Ethernet orders convert; deferred revenue +$826M in one quarter signals 2+ quarter pipeline visibility.

shifts.note: 800G AI Ethernet displacing InfiniBand in large-scale AI clusters at Meta, Microsoft, Google

shifts.trend: Deferred revenue $5.37B (Dec 2025) → $6.20B (Mar 2026) +$826M in one quarter; binding backlog $968M
shifts.note: Orders converting to committed backlog faster than shipping — demand exceeds near-term supply

shifts.trend: FY25 non-GAAP GM 64.6% → Q1 FY26 61.9% (-270bps); Q2 guide ~62%
shifts.note: New AI spine products carry higher upfront BOM — structural margin re-rating, not transient

shifts.trend: Two end customers 16%+14% FY25 (30% combined); same pattern as prior years; Cloud Titan trials diversifying
shifts.note: Concentration manageable as both hyperscalers are structurally committed to 800G/1.6T build-outs

ANET.verdict: Cloud Titan AI Ethernet ramp is the key inflection — deferred revenue acceleration is the leading indicator that FY26 guidance of $11.0-11.5B (+38-44%) is achievable. Gross margin compression to ~62% is a known trade-off for share gains, not a deterioration.

AVGO.headline: AI semiconductor revenue tracking to $40B+ FY26 as custom XPU ramp accelerates; top-5 customer concentration rising to 50% but these are hyperscalers with structurally committed AI capex.

shifts.trend: AI semiconductor: FY25 $12.2B → Q1 FY26 ~$9-10B run-rate → Q2 guide implies $10-11B; FY26 $40B+ target
shifts.note: Google TPU v6 and Amazon networking ASICs primary; two additional hyperscaler XPU wins in pipeline

shifts.trend: Top-5 end customers: ~40% FY25 → ~50% Q1 FY26; single distributor customer 42% vs 29%
shifts.note: Concentration is the thesis — Broadcom is the AI ASIC supplier of choice for hyperscalers who want to avoid NVIDIA

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 77% maintained FY25 → Q1 FY26 76.9% → Q2 guide 77%; VMware software driving
shifts.note: Software (VMware) mix prevents AI hardware dilution; best-in-class gross margin for semiconductor company

shifts.trend: Infrastructure software (VMware) $21.5B FY25 full year; subscription conversion ahead of plan; ARR building
shifts.note: VMware recurring revenue adds predictability that pure-play semiconductors lack

AVGO.verdict: Broadcom is a direct NVIDIA alternative thesis — hyperscalers use XPUs to avoid NVIDIA concentration. The FY26 $40B+ AI semiconductor target is supported by multiple contracted XPU designs. VMware provides downside cushion. High conviction.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud operating leverage 3x YoY to $6.6B as margin expansion compounds; revenue backlog surged to $467.6B in one quarter from multi-GW TPU supply deals.

shifts.note: Multi-GW TPU hardware supply agreements inflating backlog; novel revenue model for AI compute

shifts.trend: Cloud op income: FY25 $8.9B (18% margin) → Q1 2026 $6.6B (33% margin); extrapolated FY26 op income ~$26B
shifts.note: Massive operating leverage as revenue scales on fixed infrastructure base; 3x margin in 12 months

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $91.4B → FY26 $130B+ annualized; Q1 2026 alone $35.7B
shifts.note: AI infrastructure investment accelerating, not decelerating — demand visibility from $467B backlog justifies

shifts.trend: Search still 51% of revenue and +14% YoY; AI Overviews not cannibalizing Search monetization
shifts.note: Core Search resilience provides cash-flow base funding the AI infra investment

GOOGL.verdict: Google Cloud's 33% operating margin in Q1 2026 is the most important data point — it proves hyperscaler cloud margins can structurally expand as AI services layer on top of infrastructure. The $467B backlog is a multi-year demand lock-in. Thesis strengthening.

META.headline: Capex raised twice: FY25 $40B → FY26 $115-135B (Jan) → $125-145B (Apr); ad revenue resilient at +33% YoY; AI model investment compounding into engagement and monetization.

shifts.trend: FY24 capex $38.4B → FY25 $40.0B → FY26 initial $115-135B → raised $125-145B by Q1 2026
shifts.note: Largest YoY capex step-up in hyperscaler history; AI training clusters and DC for Llama 5/6 training

shifts.trend: Q4 FY25 revenue $54.9B (+27%) → Q1 2026 $56.3B (+33%); impressions +19% + price/ad +12%
shifts.note: AI-driven targeting and Reels monetization compounding — both impressions and pricing growing simultaneously

shifts.trend: Operating margin: 42% FY25 → 40.7% Q1 2026 (slight compression on RL investment); RL losses $4B/qtr
shifts.note: Core margins stable despite massive capex; RL (VR/AR) remains a long-term bet, not near-term drag concern

shifts.trend: No single advertiser >5% of revenue; advertiser base 10M+; diversified across verticals
shifts.note: Ad business inherently diversified — not a concentration risk

META.verdict: Meta is the highest-conviction hyperscaler capex story: their own AI investment directly improves their core ad product (better targeting = higher CPMs). The capex raise to $125-145B is funded by 33% ad revenue growth — this is a virtuous cycle, not a burn rate.

AMZN.headline: AWS RPO surged from $244B to $364B in 90 days after OpenAI $100B expansion; Q1 capex $43.2B (+78% YoY); AWS margin temporarily compressed but structurally intact at 37-39%.

shifts.trend: AWS RPO: $244B (Dec 2025) → $364B (Mar 2026) — $120B added in 90 days; weighted-avg life 5.5 years
shifts.note: OpenAI $100B expansion plus additional enterprise AI commitments — demand far exceeds current capacity

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $128.3B → Q1 2026 alone $43.2B (implying $170B+ FY26 annualized); +78% YoY in Q1
shifts.note: Trainium2, Graviton4, data center construction all in flight simultaneously

shifts.trend: AWS op margin: 39.4% (Q1 2025) → 37.7% (Q1 2026); FY25 37.1% vs FY24 30.5% (still expanding multi-year)
shifts.note: Near-term depreciation from capex surge compresses margin; expected to normalize H2 2026 as capacity matures

shifts.trend: AWS revenue: FY25 $107.4B (+28%) → Q1 2026 $37.6B (+28% YoY); growth rate stable at ~28%
shifts.note: RPO $364B implies AWS growth acceleration ahead as committed capacity deploys

AMZN.verdict: AWS RPO explosion to $364B is the defining data point — that's 3.4 years of current AWS revenue committed. Near-term margin compression is fully explained by accelerating capex depreciation. H2 2026 should see AWS margin recovery as utilization on new capacity builds.

MSFT.headline: Azure acceleration from +33% (Q2) to +40% (Q3) as AI services compound; commercial RPO doubled YoY to $627B; capex $80.1B first 9 months vs $40.9B prior year.

shifts.trend: Azure: +33% (Q2 FY26) → +40% (Q3 FY26); AI services growing 3-4x faster than core Azure
shifts.note: Copilot seat expansion, Foundry AI platform, and OpenAI API resale all compounding

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO: $510B (Q2 Dec 2025) → $625B (Jan 2026) → $627B (Q3 Mar 2026)
shifts.note: RPO growing faster than revenue recognizes — committed demand pipeline extending

shifts.trend: 9M FY26 capex $80.1B vs $40.9B prior year (+96% YoY); Q3 alone $30.9B — no deceleration signal
shifts.note: AI data center construction and GPU procurement at full velocity; demand justifies spend

shifts.trend: Q3 guide $71.5-72.5B → printed ~$72.9B; Q4 guide $73-75B; FY26 tracking ~$280B total
shifts.note: Consistent beat on Azure guidance; AI acceleration exceeding initial estimates each quarter

MSFT.verdict: Azure +40% is the highest single-quarter growth rate since 2021 — re-acceleration confirmed, not a mean-reversion cycle. Commercial RPO $627B provides extraordinary forward visibility. Microsoft is the best-positioned hyperscaler for enterprise AI monetization.

ORCL.headline: OCI acceleration from +52% (Q2) to +84% (Q3); RPO 4.2x YoY to $552.6B; FY27 guide $66B+ implies multi-year compounding.

shifts.trend: OCI infrastructure: Q2 $3.99B (+52%) → Q3 $4.89B (+84%); RPO $523B (Nov 2025) → $552B (Feb 2026)
shifts.note: xAI, OpenAI, Meta, MSFT (multi-cloud) bookings inflating RPO; capacity is the bottleneck not demand

shifts.trend: OCI capacity demand exceeding supply across both quarters — construction and GPU procurement pacing deployments
shifts.note: Oracle building 100+ cloud regions; AI cluster capacity additions can't keep up with booking pace

shifts.trend: Q3 guide $15.6-16.0B → printed $17.19B; Q4 guide $18.5-18.7B; FY27 guide $66B+ (from ~$65B FY26)
shifts.note: Consistent beats — guidance is conservative given capacity constraints on realized demand

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP op margin ~43-45% maintained across both quarters; OCI infrastructure margin improving as utilization scales
shifts.note: Database and cloud applications provide margin floor while OCI infrastructure margin expands

ORCL.verdict: Oracle is the sleeper hyperscaler — OCI +84% with $552B RPO is structurally larger than the market prices. The FY27 $66B+ guide implies 50%+ CAGR for OCI specifically. The investment case hinges on whether they can build capacity fast enough, not on demand.

CRM.headline: Agentforce $500M+ ACV signed H2 FY26, driving re-acceleration thesis; FY27 guidance $47.5-47.7B (+15%) vs FY25/26 ~10% marks the inflection.

shifts.trend: FY26 revenue $41.5B (+10%) → FY27 guide $47.5-47.7B (+15%); cRPO +16% in FY26 leads revenue by 1-2 quarters
shifts.note: Agentforce displacing RPA and human workflows — both net-new logos and seat expansion within existing accounts

shifts.trend: Agentforce 5,000+ customers by end FY26; $500M+ ACV signed H2 FY26; FY27 $2B+ ACV target
shifts.note: Informatica ($9.6B, Nov 2025) adds data management context — agents need clean data pipelines to function

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP op margin 33.9% FY26 → ~34% FY27 guide; FCF margin ~34%; no margin expansion sacrifice for AI investment
shifts.note: Salesforce reaching mature SaaS margin profile while funding Agentforce at scale

shifts.trend: FY26 revenue revised $37.9B → actual $41.5B (Informatica contribution); FY27 guide $47.5-47.7B
shifts.note: FY27 acceleration is first post-Agentforce guidance cycle — the key test of the re-acceleration thesis

CRM.verdict: Agentforce ACV of $500M+ in H2 FY26 is the thesis catalyst — it validates that enterprises pay incrementally for AI agents, not just existing Salesforce licenses. FY27 +15% revenue guide vs prior +10% confirms re-acceleration. Informatica integration adds data moat. Upgrading conviction.

NOW.headline: Consistent 22-25% growth with RPO $27.7B +25%; Now Assist AI agents in 15%+ of new ACV; FY26 subscription guidance raised to $15.70-15.72B.

shifts.note: Now Assist agent modules reaching 15%+ of ACV in H2 FY25; expanding AI upsell opportunity

shifts.trend: FY26 subscription $15.60-15.64B initial → raised to $15.70-15.72B after Q1 beat
shifts.note: Beat-and-raise cadence; management guidance consistently conservative

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP op margin: 28.5% FY25 → 30.2% Q1 2026; FCF $1.63B Q1 (43% margin) — best-in-class SaaS efficiency
shifts.note: Operating leverage compounding as subscription base scales; AI modules add ACV without proportional cost

shifts.trend: RPO $28.2B (Dec 2025) → $27.7B (Mar 2026) — slight sequential dip on strong Q4 recognition; QoQ within normal range
shifts.note: Multi-year contracts still renewing and expanding; no churn signal in leading indicators

NOW.verdict: ServiceNow is the most consistent software compounder in the portfolio: 20-25% growth, ~30% EBIT margin, ~43% FCF margin, and Now Assist AI upsell adding incremental ACV without new logos. FY26 subscription $15.7B guide is achievable. The risk is valuation, not business quality.

SNOW.headline: FY26 product revenue $4.5B (+31%) vs guidance upgrade; NRR 127%; Arctic LLM and Cortex AI driving consumption expansion.

shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 product rev $900M (+28%) → FY26 full year $4.5B (+31%); RPO $7.9B (Oct 25) → $9.8B (Jan 26)
shifts.note: AI/ML consumption in Snowflake (Cortex, Snowpark, Arctic) accelerating overall platform usage

shifts.trend: Forbes Global 2000 customers 790 = ~43% of revenue; NRR 127% — existing customers expanding consumption
shifts.note: Land-and-expand model intact; AI features driving expansion velocity within existing accounts

shifts.trend: Product gross margin ~74% maintained; adj op margin improving from -1% to +6% FY26 as scale builds
shifts.note: S&M spend declining as % of revenue; path to 20%+ adj op margin visible over next 2-3 years

shifts.trend: FY26 initial $3.33-3.35B product rev → actual $4.5B product rev; FY27 guide $5.1B+ (+13%) implied
shifts.note: FY26 outperformed initial guide significantly; Cortex AI consumption driving upside

SNOW.verdict: Snowflake's reacceleration from 22-28% to 31% in FY26 is driven by AI consumption (Cortex, Snowpark). NRR 127% and RPO expansion confirm the land-and-expand engine is working again. FY27 guide implies growth stabilization, not reacceleration — watch Q1/Q2 FY27 for signals.

PLTR.headline: Revenue growth re-accelerated to 85% YoY in Q1 2026; US commercial +95% on AIP; operating margin 46%; FY26 raised to $6.64-6.68B.

shifts.note: AIP bootcamp-to-production conversion rate expanding; Fortune 500 enterprises deploying at scale

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP op margin: 31% FY25 → 46% Q1 2026; operating income $176M (Q1 2025) → $754M (Q1 2026)
shifts.note: Operating leverage exceptional — revenue growing 85% while cost base grows much slower

shifts.trend: FY26 initial $6.0-6.2B → raised to $6.64-6.68B after Q1 beat; Q1 beat guidance by $100M+
shifts.note: Each quarter bringing material guidance increase; US commercial AIP continues surprising to upside

shifts.trend: FCF $626M in Q1 2026 (38% margin); GAAP profitable 4+ consecutive quarters; S&P 500 inclusion
shifts.note: GAAP profitability and S&P 500 inclusion driving institutional accumulation

PLTR.verdict: Palantir is experiencing its own hyper-growth inflection — AIP is driving US commercial to near-100% growth rates. The 46% operating margin proves this is not a 'grow at all costs' mode. FY26 raise to $6.64-6.68B is likely conservative given current trajectory. Strongest momentum in software portfolio.

CDNS.headline: Revenue growing 17-19% with $8.0B backlog; China +36% YoY despite BIS license headwind; FY26 guidance raised to $6.22-6.28B.

shifts.trend: FY25 $5.27B (+14%) → FY26 guide $6.22-6.28B (+18%); China $189M Q1 (+36% YoY despite BIS)
shifts.note: AI chip design complexity (3nm, 2nm, gate-all-around) adding EDA verification steps per design

shifts.trend: China 11% → 13% of revenue despite BIS license requirement for advanced nodes imposed May 2025
shifts.note: Cadence expects 'limited impact' — legacy node work exempted; advanced node licensing delayed not blocked

shifts.trend: Backlog $7.8B (Dec 2025) → $8.0B (Mar 2026); 55% recognized in next 12 months
shifts.note: Backlog growing slower than revenue — healthy sign; not front-loading deals ahead of restrictions

shifts.trend: FY26 initial $6.18-6.26B → raised to $6.22-6.28B after Q1 beat; consistent beat pattern
shifts.note: EDA demand driven by fabless AI chip proliferation — each new AI ASIC design is a multi-year Cadence engagement

CDNS.verdict: Cadence is the EDA infrastructure play on AI chip proliferation — every hyperscaler custom ASIC, every startup AI chip uses Cadence tools for years. China BIS risk (~$100-150M) is manageable and partly mitigated by non-restricted work. Revenue growth reaccelerating to 18% confirms AI chip design activity expanding. Steady compounder.

SNPS.headline: Ansys closes ($35B, Jan 2025); FY26 revenue $9.97-10.03B guided (+47%); Ansys D&A compresses GAAP EPS but cash earnings and cross-sell synergies intact.

shifts.note: Ansys contribution is additive, not replacing organic EDA growth; cross-sell synergies beginning

shifts.trend: Design IP $1.22B (+32% FY25) — silicon IP licensing boom as fabless players pre-license blocks for AI chips
shifts.note: IP business has highest margins and lowest churn in Synopsys portfolio

shifts.trend: BIS export license for EDA to China advanced nodes (May 2025); $100-150M FY26 revenue at risk
shifts.note: Risk manageable but persistent — cannot be fully mitigated by routing through third-party channel

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP op margin 36% FY25 → ~25% Q1 FY26 on $394M Ansys amortization; cash EPS ~$2.70 vs GAAP EPS ~$0.60
shifts.note: GAAP margin compressed by D&A, not underlying economics — focus on FCF margin and non-GAAP EPS

SNPS.verdict: Ansys is an exceptional strategic acquisition — EDA + simulation creates a combined platform that no competitor can replicate. GAAP EPS compression from D&A is expected and will normalize over 10+ years. The real metric is non-GAAP EPS and FCF, both growing double-digits. China BIS risk is the only meaningful uncertainty.

INTC.headline: DCAI re-accelerating +22% on premium mix; Intel Foundry losing $12B/yr with 18A node at risk without external win; $4.1B Q1 restructuring signals deeper cost cut.

shifts.trend: DCAI: FY25 $18.1B (+9%) → Q1 2026 $5.1B (+22% YoY); ASPs +27% from Granite Rapids premium pricing
shifts.note: DCAI re-accelerating despite supply constraints — market demand exceeded available product supply in Q1

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry: FY25 -$12-13B operating loss → Q1 2026 -$2.4B (widening on 18A ramp costs); external revenue $644M total FY25
shifts.note: 18A viability depends on landing major external customer by mid-2026; no public win announced yet

shifts.trend: Q1 2026 GAAP net loss $3.7B on $4.1B restructuring; 15,000+ headcount reduction; FY25 net loss included impairments
shifts.note: Balance sheet impaired but manageable; $10B cost reduction target by 2027 essential for Foundry viability

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: ~40% FY25 → Q1 2026 guide ~38-40%; Foundry losses in external segment weigh on consolidated margins
shifts.note: Client and DCAI product GMs are healthy (~50%+); Foundry drag is the culprit

INTC.verdict: Intel is a restructuring story, not a growth story yet. DCAI at +22% is encouraging but not enough to offset Foundry losses. The critical binary: 18A external customer (if won = thesis-changing; if not = 18A pause). Gaudi 3 AI accelerator needs a major hyperscaler win to change the AI narrative.

KLAC.headline: Record revenues two consecutive quarters; DRAM/HBM driving Korea +80% YoY; Q4 guide $3.55-3.85B implies sequential re-acceleration into memory upcycle.

shifts.trend: Q2 FY26 $3.08B (+10%) → Q3 FY26 $3.42B (+11%); Q4 guide midpoint $3.70B (+15%); record revenues each quarter
shifts.note: HBM inspection intensity (patterned wafer + overlay) compounding as 8-hi and 12-hi HBM stacks scale

shifts.trend: Korea revenue +80% YoY Q3 FY26 on SK Hynix and Samsung HBM WFE surge; lead times for KLA tools extending
shifts.note: Every HBM die requires KPCI overlay metrology at 8-10 critical layers vs 3-4 for standard DRAM

shifts.trend: China: 24% (Q2) → 19% (Q3) of revenue — declining as export controls divert orders to Korea/Taiwan
shifts.note: China decline offset by Korea and N. America strength; net WFE still growing

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 61.5% (Q2) → 62.0% (Q3); Q4 guide 62%; service mix increasing (23% of revenue Q3)
shifts.note: Service revenue (23%) growing faster than systems — adds recurring component to WFE exposure

KLAC.verdict: KLA is the clearest HBM beneficiary in WFE — inspection intensity per HBM wafer is 3-4x standard DRAM, and HBM capacity is growing 50%+ annually through 2028. Korea +80% YoY confirms SK Hynix and Samsung are spending heavily. Q4 guide $3.70B is the next record. High conviction.

LRCX.headline: Memory mix rising from 34% to 39% in two quarters; HBM etch content 2-3x standard DRAM per layer; Q4 guide $5.90-6.50B continues WFE upcycle.

shifts.note: HBM etch step count (10+ high-AR steps per layer vs 3-4 standard DRAM) driving content-per-wafer expansion

shifts.trend: Memory mix: 31% (Q1 FY26) → 34% (Q2) → 39% (Q3); DRAM and HBM share growing each quarter
shifts.note: Tight HBM supply = more equipment orders from MU, SK Hynix, Samsung; no sign of slowing

shifts.trend: China revenue declining: ~25% (Q1) → 21% (Q2) → 19% (Q3) — export controls gradually reducing exposure
shifts.note: Non-China growth (Korea, Taiwan, US) fully offsetting China decline — diversification improving

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 47.0% (Q2) → 47.5% (Q3); customer-support revenue growing faster than systems (more recurring)
shifts.note: Gross margin ~47-48% is LRCX structural range; customer-support recurring revenue mix improving

LRCX.verdict: Lam Research is a direct HBM play: every additional HBM layer requires 10+ high-aspect-ratio etch steps that only LRCX and TEL can supply. Memory mix rising from 34% to 39% in two quarters confirms HBM WFE is accelerating. Q4 guide $5.90-6.50B continues the upcycle.

AMAT.headline: Memory spending rising on HBM/DRAM; Q2 guide $7.50-7.90B; China declining from 28% to ~20%; gross margin expanding toward 49%.

shifts.trend: FY25 (Oct 25) revenue $27.2B (+6%) → Q1 FY26 $7.17B (+7%); memory customer spending higher YoY on HBM
shifts.note: HBM deposition (ALD, CVD), planarization (CMP), and etch all gaining from memory intensity increase

shifts.trend: China: 28% FY25 → ~20% Q1 FY26 — export controls shifting China customers to legacy node equipment
shifts.note: Korea and Taiwan offsetting China decline; TSMC N2/A16 and Samsung HBM compensating

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: 47.5% FY25 → 48.8% Q1 FY26 → ~49% Q2 guide
shifts.note: Service revenue (AGS) growing 5%+ — higher-margin recurring business increasing as installed base ages

shifts.trend: WFE market +10-15% in CY2026 per industry estimates; AMAT tracking in line with market growth
shifts.note: GAA/nanosheet transition at TSMC N2 incrementally positive for AMAT CVD and implant content

AMAT.verdict: Applied Materials is a balanced WFE play — memory (HBM), logic (TSMC N2), and foundry. China exposure declining from 28% to 20% reduces geopolitical risk. Gross margin expansion to ~49% is durable. Q2 guide $7.70B midpoint implies ~10% YoY growth — inline with WFE market.

MRVL.headline: Data center accelerating from 74% of revenue to FY27 $10B+ target; custom AI ASIC ramp for Google and Amazon; CPO 1.6T platform entering production 2026-2027.

shifts.note: Custom XPU ramp (Google TPU v6 + Amazon Trainium2 networking) driving step-change in Q4/Q1 FY27

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: 51% FY26 avg → 58% Q3 FY26 → 60%+ Q4 FY26 guide; Q1 FY27 guide ~60.5%
shifts.note: Custom ASIC mix (higher ASP, design-win sticky) structurally re-rating gross margin from ~41% FY25

shifts.trend: Data center: Q3 $1.08B (71% of mix) → FY26 $6.1B (74%) → FY27 $10B+ target; CPO EO 1.6T platform sampling
shifts.note: Co-Packaged Optics with Marvell EO chiplets = future switch+optics integration for 1.6T AI fabric

shifts.trend: Three customers = 70% of AR; Distributor A = 37% of total revenue; hyperscaler-led XPU reliance growing
shifts.note: Concentration risk mitigated by multi-year program commitments from hyperscalers

MRVL.verdict: Marvell is the custom AI ASIC compounder — Google and Amazon are captive multi-year customers. The gross margin re-rating from 41% (FY25) to 60%+ (Q4 FY26) is structural, driven by XPU mix. FY27 $10B data center target is achievable with current ramp trajectory. CPO EO platform adds the next growth layer.

HPE.headline: Networking +152% on Juniper drives margin expansion; AI server backlog growing despite supply constraints; FY26 adj EPS raised to $2.35-2.55.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue $32.7B (+9%) → Q1 FY26 $9.3B (+18%); Networking +151% Q1 on full-quarter Juniper contribution
shifts.note: Juniper brings AI campus switching and data center fabric — differentiated from HPE's prior server-centric model

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: 34.5% FY25 → 35.9% Q1 FY26 (+140bps); Juniper software drives mix improvement
shifts.note: Juniper software and networking margin profile significantly above HPE blended average

shifts.trend: FY26 adj EPS initial $2.15-2.35 → raised to $2.35-2.55 after Q1 beat; Juniper synergies ahead of plan
shifts.note: Synergy target $300M by 2027; ahead of pace after first full quarter of Juniper integration

shifts.trend: AI servers (Cloud & AI segment) growing 71% but capacity-constrained as GPU allocation limits production
shifts.note: AI server supply constrained by GPU availability from NVIDIA; demand exceeds HPE's buildable supply

HPE.verdict: Juniper transforms HPE's growth and margin profile — networking is the highest-margin, fastest-growing segment and Juniper integration is ahead of plan. AI server growth 71% is supply-constrained, not demand-constrained. FY26 guide raise confirms the thesis. The risk: Juniper networking competition from Arista and Cisco.

GEV.headline: RPO surged from $110B (FY24) → $150B (Dec 2025) → $163B (Mar 2026) — data center power demand driving Gas Power order records; adj EBITDA guidance raised to $2.5-3.0B.

shifts.trend: Total RPO: $110B (FY24) → $150.4B (Dec 2025) → $163.3B (Mar 2026); Gas Power RPO $99.7B; 3-year backlog coverage
shifts.note: Data center power demand driving hyperscaler pre-purchases of multi-GW gas turbine capacity 2027-2030

shifts.trend: FY25 Gas Power orders record; Q1 2026 HA-Turbine orders 12 vs 8 prior year; Electrification +25% Q1
shifts.note: Both generation (turbines) and grid hardware (transformers, switchgear) growing on AI data center demand

shifts.trend: FY26 adj EBITDA: initial $2.0-2.5B → raised to $2.5-3.0B after Q1 beat; Gas Power margin >20% adj
shifts.note: First year of meaningful EBITDA profitability; restructured Wind segment losses declining

shifts.trend: Wind: exiting offshore, rationalizing onshore; losses narrowing from -$2B FY24 to ~-$0.5B FY26 target
shifts.note: Wind drag on consolidated profitability declining; Gas Power and Electrification carrying the company

GEV.verdict: GE Vernova is the 'power the AI boom' thesis in the most direct form: hyperscalers are ordering 3-5GW gas turbine capacity multi-year ahead. RPO $163B at a company with $34B annual revenue = 5 years of backlog. EBITDA guide raise confirms profitability inflection. High conviction on power infrastructure theme.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog +44% YoY to $14.5B; organic orders +42%; FY26 adj EPS guidance raised to $14.00-14.40; data center demand extending visibility 2+ years.

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas backlog: ~$10.0B (FY25) → $14.5B Q1 2026 (+44% YoY, +32% organic); book-to-bill 1.2x
shifts.note: Demand accelerating faster than capacity can be added — lead times extending into 2027-2028

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas Q1: +20% YoY; Electrical Global Q1: +21%; organic orders +42% in Americas
shifts.note: Data center: switchgear, PDU, busway, UPS — all product lines growing double-digits

shifts.trend: FY26 adj EPS: initial ~$13.50-14.00 → raised to $14.00-14.40 after Q1; organic revenue guidance +12-14%
shifts.note: Eaton tends to guide conservatively; Q1 beat suggests further raises likely through FY26

shifts.trend: Acquired Boyd Thermal (liquid cooling data center systems) Q1 2026; expanding from power distribution into direct liquid cooling
shifts.note: Strategic: Eaton becoming end-to-end data center power + cooling supplier, deepening moat

ETN.verdict: Eaton is a high-quality compounder with a 2+ year demand backlog. The +42% organic orders growth in Electrical Americas is one of the strongest order signals in the infrastructure universe. Boyd Thermal acquisition extends the moat into DLC. FY26 EPS raise is likely not the last.

VRT.headline: Backlog $15B (+108% YoY) entering FY26; Americas margins 27% in Q1 2026; FY26 adj EPS raised to $4.35-4.55; DLC orders growing 3-4x as GB200 cooling requirements drive design shifts.

shifts.trend: Order backlog: $7.2B (Dec 2024) → $15.0B (Dec 2025) → record again Q1 2026; 1-year RPO growing each quarter
shifts.note: Hyperscaler liquid cooling commitments multi-year: GB200 NVL72 racks require rear-door/immersion solutions
Americas is the leading edge of AI data center build (hyperscalers primarily US-sited) — Vertiv's strongest region

shifts.trend: Americas margin: 22% FY25 → 27.0% Q1 2026; consolidated GM 35.4% FY25 → 37.7% Q1 2026 (+400bps)
shifts.note: Americas margin expansion 500bps in two quarters on volume leverage and mix — not yet at ceiling

shifts.trend: FY26 adj EPS: $4.20-4.40 initial → $4.35-4.55 after Q1; organic revenue growth guidance +25-30%
shifts.note: Each quarter bringing EPS guide raise; backlog provides high confidence in FY26-FY27 delivery

VRT.verdict: Vertiv is the clearest DLC/thermal management play on AI hardware. Americas margin expansion to 27% in Q1 demonstrates exceptional pricing power. Backlog $15B at $9.15B annual revenue = 1.6-year coverage. FY26 raise confirms trajectory. The risk is execution at scale — $425-525M capex must translate to delivery capacity.

CRWV.headline: RPO surged from $60.7B (Dec 2025) to $98.8B (Mar 2026) in 90 days; Microsoft concentration fell to 45%; revenue +112%; raised $7.8B post-quarter to fund $20B capex plan.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue $4.1B (+258% YoY) → Q1 2026 $2.08B (+112% YoY); Q2 guidance ~$2.8-3.0B implies FY26 ~$10B+
shifts.note: Scale inflection underway: $1.15B FY24 → $4.1B FY25 → $10B+ FY26 trajectory

shifts.trend: Microsoft: 72% FY25 → 45% Q1 2026; Meta emerges at 20% Q1 2026; additional hyperscaler customers forming
shifts.note: Diversification progressing faster than expected; Meta as Customer B validates business model beyond MSFT

shifts.trend: RPO: $60.7B (Dec 2025) → $98.8B (Mar 2026) in 90 days — $38B added; 36% recognized in next 24 months
shifts.note: Committed demand growing 2x faster than revenue; capacity the bottleneck for recognizing contracted value

shifts.trend: Net loss $3.0B FY25, $740M Q1 2026; accumulated deficit $3.4B; $23B+ debt on balance sheet
shifts.note: Interest + D&A = $2.5B/qtr burden at scale; needs revenue to reach $12B+ to break even on net income

CRWV.verdict: CoreWeave is a high-risk, high-reward neocloud: RPO $98.8B is extraordinary visibility for a ~$10B revenue company. Microsoft concentration falling to 45% is crucial de-risking. The $23B debt load and $3B+ annual loss require continued capital access — rates matter for this business. High upside, high balance-sheet risk.

CEG.headline: Calpine close Jan 7 creates world's largest private power company (55 GW); combined adj EBITDA guidance $10.5-11.5B for FY26; nuclear CPPA pipeline growing on hyperscaler 24/7 carbon-free demand.

shifts.trend: Standalone CEG FY25 adj EBITDA ~$4.5-5.0B → combined FY26 guidance $10.5-11.5B (+130%); Calpine 23 GW additive
shifts.note: Calpine acquisition transforms scale; gas generation adds despatchable capacity that nuclear alone can't provide

shifts.trend: Pre-acquisition: 100% nuclear/clean; post-Calpine: 60% nuclear/clean + 40% gas; retail accounts 2.5M
shifts.note: Diversification reduces single-fuel risk; retail cross-sell opportunity between nuclear CPPA and gas supply

shifts.trend: Nuclear capacity factor ~95% both pre/post acquisition; Crane (TMI Unit 1) operational at 835 MW
shifts.note: Nuclear baseload operation unchanged — the core asset; Calpine adds peaker/following capacity

shifts.trend: Dividend growth: 10%/yr target; $1.5B buyback authorized; DOE $1.0B loan for Crane life extension
shifts.note: Capital returns accelerating post-close as combined cash flow supports higher distributions

CEG.verdict: Constellation post-Calpine is the most direct play on hyperscaler 24/7 carbon-free power demand. Nuclear capacity factor 95% delivers premium CPPA pricing of $25-40/MWh above market. Combined EBITDA $10.5-11.5B at ~10-12x EV/EBITDA is not expensive for contracted power generation. Integration risk is the main uncertainty.

CAT.headline: Power & Energy +8% FY25 → +22% Q1 2026 as data center genset orders compound; backlog at record; tariff exposure $2.2-2.4B manageable; FY26 guide low double-digit growth.

shifts.trend: P&E: FY25 $25.9B (+8%) → Q1 2026 $7.0B (+22% YoY); data center genset orders extending 6+ quarters
shifts.note: Power generation for data centers (2-20MW standby/prime gensets) is the fastest-growing end-market

shifts.trend: P&E backlog growing each quarter; genset lead times 6-8 quarters; hyperscalers pre-committing 2026-2028 capacity
shifts.note: CAT supply chain constrained by turbine components and electrical switchgear; pricing power intact

shifts.trend: Tariff costs: $2.2-2.4B FY26 guidance; partially offset by pricing (+$108M P&E Q1) and supply chain shifts
shifts.note: CAT has global manufacturing; can shift some production but full tariff mitigation takes 12-18 months

shifts.trend: FY26 guide: low double-digit revenue growth vs $64.8B FY25; maintained unchanged through Q1 despite tariffs
shifts.note: Conservative guide — P&E strength can offset tariff drag if backlog deploys on schedule

CAT.verdict: Caterpillar is the infrastructure-grade power play: military-spec reliability, global service network, and 6-8 quarter data center backlog visibility. P&E re-accelerating to +22% in Q1 is the key signal. Tariff risk is manageable and partially mitigated. Consistent beat-and-raise company.

CMI.headline: Power Systems EBITDA margin expanded 590bps to 29.5% in Q1 2026; data center order visibility 6-8 quarters forward; FY26 guide raises adj EBITDA to 15.5-16.0%.

shifts.trend: Power Systems EBITDA: 23.6% (Q1 2025) → 29.5% (Q1 2026), +590bps on volume leverage; consolidated guide 15.5-16.0%
shifts.note: Power Generation product mix (data center vs industrial) enriching — data center gensets at premium ASPs

shifts.trend: Power Systems: FY25 $7.2B (+12%) → Q1 2026 $1.96B (+19% YoY); Power Generation product +28%
shifts.note: Data center genset demand compounding; North America and China both strong; multi-quarter order book

shifts.trend: Data center order visibility 6-8 quarters forward; hyperscalers pre-committing capacity into 2027-2028
shifts.note: Cummins constrained by component supply (turbochargers, aftertreatment) more than final assembly

shifts.trend: Engine segment -3% FY25 on truck/off-highway normalization; Accelera (new energy) -10% in investment phase
shifts.note: Non-Power Systems segments not a concern — Power is 20% of revenue but growing to be 25%+

CMI.verdict: Cummins' Power Systems margin expansion to 29.5% in Q1 is exceptional — this is operating leverage translating directly to FCF. The 6-8 quarter data center order visibility provides multi-year earnings confidence. Accelera losses are a known quantity. The thesis rests on Power Systems continuing to outgrow the corporate average.

GNRC.headline: C&I growing from +23% FY25 to +28% Q1 2026 on data center demand; FY26 adj EPS raised to $9.00-9.50; Enercon acquisition adds hyperscale-specific UPS capability.

shifts.trend: C&I: FY25 $1.89B (+23%) → Q1 2026 $510M (+28% YoY); data center backlog extends into 2027-2028
shifts.note: Custom 2-5MW generator sets for hyperscale campuses — lead times 18-24 months creating sticky order book

shifts.trend: Adj EBITDA margin: 15.2% FY25 → 16.5% FY26 Q1 (18.5%); FY26 guide 18-19%
shifts.note: C&I premium pricing on custom data center units at higher margins than residential standby products

shifts.trend: Enercon acquisition (closed Q1 2026): $200M+ annualized revenue in hyperscale-specific UPS/modular power
shifts.note: Enercon expands Generac into critical power (UPS) — adjacency to their generator business, same hyperscaler customer base

shifts.trend: FY26 adj EPS: $8.40-8.90 initial → $9.00-9.50 after Q1 beat; C&I revenue guide raised to +20-25%
shifts.note: Data center data center demand sustaining despite macro uncertainty — infrastructure not discretionary

GNRC.verdict: Generac is a pure-play data center power story now that C&I is 48% of revenue and growing at 28%. Enercon adds the critical power (UPS) capability that hyperscalers increasingly specify alongside gensets. FY26 guide raise to $9.00-9.50 EPS is backed by multi-year backlog. Resi flat is not a concern.

PWR.headline: Backlog surged from $44B to $48.5B in one quarter; Electric Power +57% YoY in Q1; FY26 adj EPS raised to $11.00-11.50 on data center grid interconnection demand.

shifts.note: Data center HV substation and transmission interconnection awards adding to already-record backlog

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue $26.3B (+17%) → Q1 2026 $7.87B (+26%); Electric Power segment +57% YoY Q1
shifts.note: Hyperscaler AI campus electrical: HV substations, transmission lines, HV distribution — Electric segment driver

shifts.trend: Operating margin: 3.8% FY25 → 4.3% Q1 2026; Electric Power higher-margin than utilities work
shifts.note: Electric mix growing as % of revenue — structural margin improvement underway

shifts.trend: FY26 adj EPS: $10.50-11.00 initial → $11.00-11.50 after Q1; revenue guide raised to ~$30B
shifts.note: Backlog $48.5B at $30B annual revenue = 1.6-year coverage; management has high visibility confidence

PWR.verdict: Quanta Services is the electrical infrastructure contractor of choice for AI data center interconnection. Backlog +10% in a single quarter to $48.5B is an extraordinary leading indicator. Electric Power segment growing at 57% YoY confirms data center work is driving a step-change. FY26 EPS raise is well-supported.

NVT.headline: Systems Protection +76% Q1 2026 with 22.7% segment margin; FY26 adj EPS raised to $4.30-4.45; DLC orders growing >50% YoY as AI rack thermal requirements intensify.

shifts.trend: Systems Protection: FY25 $1.85B (+38%) → Q1 2026 $895M (+76% YoY, +50% organic); FY26 guide organic +20-25%
shifts.note: AI rack enclosures and direct liquid cooling door solutions growing faster than prior guide — hyperscale and colo both

shifts.trend: Systems Protection margin: 22.1% FY25 → 22.7% Q1 2026 (+220bps); consolidated +200bps trajectory
shifts.note: Volume leverage and pricing power combining; Trachte acquisition adding higher-margin prefab enclosures

shifts.trend: FY26 adj EPS: $3.90-4.10 initial → $4.30-4.45 after Q1; organic revenue guidance +20-25%
shifts.note: Q1 beat was substantial — implies further raise potential through FY26 if demand holds

shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$120M for DLC manufacturing capacity; Trachte prefab enclosure capacity scaling
shifts.note: Capex modest relative to growth — asset-light model preserved; outsourcing most manufacturing

NVT.verdict: nVent's Systems Protection +76% with 22.7% margin in Q1 2026 is one of the strongest quarterly prints in the cooling/enclosure space. DLC orders growing >50% confirms liquid cooling adoption is accelerating ahead of GB200/GB300 deployments. The FY26 raise from $3.90-4.10 to $4.30-4.45 EPS is substantial. High conviction.

HUBB.headline: Consistent organic growth 6-10% both segments; data center electrical and T&D grid both sustaining; FY26 adj EPS guide $19.30-19.90 reaffirmed with upside from data center acceleration.

shifts.trend: Electrical Solutions organic: +13% FY25 → +10.6% Q1 2026; Utility Solutions organic: +10% FY25 → +6.8% Q1 2026
shifts.note: Growth normalizing from peak but well above long-term average; data center and T&D both sustaining

shifts.trend: Adj op margin 19.2% FY25 → 19.5% Q1 2026; pricing mid-single-digit on both segments; volume mid-single-digit
shifts.note: Hubbell has consistent mid-to-high-single-digit organic growth + 19-20% adj margin — durable model

shifts.trend: FY26 adj EPS $19.30-19.90 reaffirmed after Q1; no upward revision yet — typically conservative on guidance
shifts.note: Hubbell guides conservatively; Q1 beat suggests end-year likely above mid-range of guide

shifts.trend: No significant supply constraints disclosed; manufacturing capacity adequate for current organic growth
shifts.note: Hubbell's product complexity (wiring devices, fittings) less capacity-constrained than power infrastructure peers

HUBB.verdict: Hubbell is the steady compounder in the power infrastructure theme — not the fastest grower, but extremely consistent: 6-11% organic growth, 19-20% adj op margins, limited tariff risk, and dual drivers (data center + T&D). FY26 guide reaffirmation is a floor; typical upward revision pattern expected.

CLS.headline: Revenue +53% YoY in Q1 2026; HPS +63% to $1.7B (42% of total); FY26 guide raised to $17-18B; hyperscaler AI cluster manufacturing at industrial scale.

shifts.note: Enterprise (AI/ML compute cluster assembly for hyperscaler) growing 101% YoY Q1 — visible build program inflecting

shifts.trend: FY26: initial $15-16B → raised to $17-18B after Q1 beat; adj EPS $6.80-7.20; adj EBITDA margin 8.0-8.5%
shifts.note: Rapid guidance raise cycle in 2025-2026; manufacturing at scale for hyperscaler AI cluster programs

shifts.trend: Adj EBITDA margin 7.4% FY25 → 8.0% Q1 2026; adj EPS $0.88 Q1 2025 → ~$1.42 Q1 2026
shifts.note: Volume leverage on fixed overhead; HPS programs at scale improving utilization

shifts.trend: Revolver upsized $750M to $1.75B Q1 2026; working capital needs growing proportionally with revenue
shifts.note: Revenue growing faster than balance sheet, but hyperscaler customers have strong payment terms

CLS.verdict: Celestica is one of the largest direct beneficiaries of hyperscaler AI capex — they are literally assembling the compute clusters. HPS at 42% of revenue growing 63% YoY is the defining metric. FY26 guide raise from $15-16B to $17-18B in one quarter shows how quickly the business is scaling. The primary risk is customer concentration.

AMKR.headline: All four end markets growing; capex $225M in Q1 2026 (+181%) as Arizona facility construction accelerates; gross margin recovery from trough on utilization improvement.

shifts.trend: FY25 $6.97B (+6%, recovery year) → Q1 2026 $1.68B (+27.5% YoY); all four segments growing simultaneously
shifts.note: Communications (premium smartphone), automotive (ADAS), computing (AI accelerators) all contributing

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $400M → FY26 $700-800M; Q1 2026 $225M alone (vs $80M Q1 2025); Arizona Phase 1 primary
shifts.note: Arizona facility ($2B total) brings US-domestic advanced packaging capacity — geopolitical moat against TSMC concentration

shifts.trend: Gross margin: ~12% trough FY25 → 14.2% Q1 2026 (+2.3pts); target 14.5-15% Q2 on continued utilization recovery
shifts.note: Margin recovery as factories fill; advanced packaging mix (higher-margin) increasing

shifts.trend: Advanced packaging (SiP, CoWoS-equivalent) capacity in tight demand; TSMC overflow and direct wins growing
shifts.note: AI accelerator packaging demand from fabless customers overflowing CoWoS at TSMC to contract options like Amkor

AMKR.verdict: Amkor is a recovery play with a strategic moat being built: Arizona advanced packaging gives US-domestic SiP and 2.5D capability that geopolitical tensions make increasingly valuable. The 27.5% revenue growth in Q1 confirms the cycle turn. Watch Arizona ramp timing and yield in H2 2026.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data center revenue growing $130M+ QoQ; Reverse Morris Trust spin of Performance Technologies announced; MOD becomes pure-play DC thermal management post-spin.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions: Q2 FY26 $470M (+41%) → Q3 FY26 $545M (+51%); 9M data center product revenue +$222M YoY
shifts.note: Data center hyperscale and colocation thermal (liquid cooling, precision air) growing 40-50% each quarter

shifts.trend: Jan 2026: Reverse Morris Trust announced — PT (automotive thermal) spinning to merge with Gentherm; MOD keeps Climate Solutions
shifts.note: Post-spin: MOD becomes pure-play data center thermal management — potential for significant multiple re-rating

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions adj EBITDA margin ~16%; consolidating with PT which is lower-margin — post-spin margin improves
shifts.note: Pure-play Climate Solutions should trade at ~12-15x EBITDA premium vs current blended multiple

shifts.trend: Manufacturing inefficiencies on data center product ramp cited Q2-Q3 FY26 — capacity scaling to meet demand
shifts.note: Quality and throughput temporarily impacted by volume ramp; management expects normalization H2 FY26

MOD.verdict: Modine is a spin-driven re-rating story: post-Reverse Morris Trust, MOD = pure-play data center cooling at $1.8B+ revenue growing 40-50% annually. Climate Solutions at 16% EBITDA margin and accelerating deserves a premium multiple that the current blended structure masks. Execution on the PT/Gentherm transaction is the key watchpoint.

LEU.headline: Backlog $3.8B → $3.9B; SWU spot $200 (+488% from 2018 low); Fluor EPC signed for Piketon expansion; Oklo JV adds HALEU fuel supply chain role.

shifts.trend: SWU spot price $200/SWU maintained Q4 2025 → Q1 2026; no supply relief visible — Russia suspension permanent
shifts.note: Structural tightness: EU/US dependency on Russian SWU eliminated; US domestic enrichment capacity 2-3 years to ramp

shifts.trend: Backlog: $2.3B contingent (FY25) → $3.9B total (Q1 2026, $3.1B LEU); Piketon EPC start 2026
shifts.note: Backlog growing as utilities seek SWU supply security; Centrus winning new contracts at high spot prices

shifts.trend: Piketon EPC contract signed with Fluor Feb 2026; construction 2026-2028; DOE HOC $700M ceiling ongoing
shifts.note: Capital deployment accelerating for enrichment capacity; DOE cost-sharing reduces Centrus risk

shifts.trend: Oklo JV (Mar 2026): HALEU deconversion JV; Technical Solutions growing +46% Q1 2026 on DOE/government work
shifts.note: HALEU deconversion = pre-processing HEU to HALEU for advanced reactor fuel; critical bottleneck Centrus can unlock

LEU.verdict: Centrus is the most directly-positioned nuclear fuel infrastructure play: SWU spot $200 creates durable pricing power, Piketon construction starts in 2026, and the Oklo HALEU JV places Centrus in the advanced reactor fuel supply chain. Revenue recognition is lumpy (delivery schedule), but the $3.9B backlog provides confidence in multi-year revenue.

OKLO.headline: Meta prepayment agreement for Oklo power (Jan 2026) is the first major hyperscaler fission offtake; ARC 300 Aurora license progressing at NRC; Centrus HALEU JV addresses fuel supply.

shifts.trend: Pre-revenue all three periods; operating expenses ~$50-60M/quarter; cash burn accelerating as headcount grows
shifts.note: Business activity inflecting: Meta prepayment, Centrus JV, NRC submissions — commercial milestones accumulating

shifts.trend: HALEU fuel supply = critical bottleneck; Centrus JV (Mar 2026) positions Oklo to secure HALEU deconversion capacity
shifts.note: Fuel supply agreement with Centrus is existential de-risking — advanced reactor without HALEU can't operate

shifts.trend: NRC ARC 300 license application progressing; Trump-era NRC reform reducing review timelines for advanced reactors
shifts.note: Political tailwind: current administration strongly supportive of advanced nuclear; NRC staffing for reviews increasing

shifts.trend: Cash burn ~$200M/year; equity/PIPE financings diluting; no revenue until first Aurora commercial operation (~2028-2030)
shifts.note: High dilution risk at pre-revenue stage; need to evaluate cost of capital vs optionality value

OKLO.verdict: Oklo is a long-duration optionality bet: if ARC 300 licenses and operates commercially by 2028-2030, the Meta/hyperscaler offtake pipeline is transformational. The Centrus HALEU JV is a major de-risking milestone. However, this is a pre-revenue, high-dilution situation — appropriate position sizing is critical.

FN.headline: DCI growing +90% in two quarters; FQ3 revenue $1.21B +39% YoY; 1.6T hyperscaler interconnect ramp driving FQ4 guidance $1.25-1.30B.

shifts.note: 400ZR+ to 800ZR module transition driving DCI volume; hyperscaler AI campus requires ultra-high-speed optical interconnect

shifts.trend: Telecom $431M (+43% FQ3) surpassed Datacom as largest line; HPC/Silicon Photonics $107M growing
shifts.note: Product mix diversifying across optical categories — Fabrinet manufacturing breadth reducing single-segment risk

shifts.trend: Gross margin 11.7% (FQ2) → 11.9% (FQ3) +20bps; FQ4 guide ~12% — slow but steady improvement
shifts.note: Contract manufacturing margin floor ~11-12%; volume leverage gradually improving; Thailand FTA limits tariff exposure

shifts.trend: FQ3 guide $1.10-1.15B → printed $1.21B (+5% beat); FQ4 guide $1.25-1.30B; FY26 implied ~$4.55B
shifts.note: Consistent beats as DCI and Telecom both surprise; 1.6T ramp is the next catalyst for FY27

FN.verdict: Fabrinet is the optical manufacturing backbone for the 800G/1.6T hyperscaler interconnect cycle. DCI +90% YoY is exceptional for a contract manufacturer. The gross margin story is modest (11-12%) but volume scale drives operating leverage. Thailand manufacturing location limits tariff risk. 1.6T ramp in FQ4-FQ1 FY27 is the next catalyst.

COHR.headline: D&C segment +34% → +41% YoY two quarters; gross margin re-rating to 38%+; FQ4 guidance $1.90-2.00B; 1.6T product entering hyperscaler qualification.

shifts.note: 800G volume expanding; 1.6T in hyperscaler qualification — product cadence visible through FY27

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: FQ2 37.5% → FQ3 38% (+50bps QoQ); D&C segment margin ~25-26%; FY26 target 40%
shifts.note: Industrial divestiture (Munich) and 800G volume pricing together driving structural margin re-rating

shifts.trend: Industrial segment declining from divestiture (-16% FQ3); D&C 75% of revenue growing 40%+
shifts.note: Portfolio simplification to pure-play photonics/optics supporting both multiple and margin expansion

shifts.trend: FQ3 guide $1.75-1.85B → printed $1.81B in range; FQ4 guide $1.90-2.00B; FY26 trajectory toward $7B
shifts.note: Sequential revenue growth maintained each quarter as 800G ramp compounds

COHR.verdict: Coherent is the best gross-margin re-rating story in optics: 38% now → 40%+ as 1.6T adds to 800G base. D&C growing 40%+ two consecutive quarters confirms AI datacenter optical demand is structural. Munich divestiture removes drag. FQ4 guide $1.90-2.00B implies FY26 run-rate $7B+. Compelling.

LITE.headline: Revenue +99% → +90% YoY two quarters; gross margin re-rating 1,600bps; FQ4 guidance $850-900M; 1.6T coherent product qualification underway.

shifts.trend: FQ2 $741M (+99% YoY) → FQ3 $808M (+90%); 9M FY26 revenue $2.3B vs $1.2B prior year
shifts.note: Near-triple-digit growth rate normalizing to high double-digits; base effect growing — underlying demand intact
Structural re-rating not transient: 800G cloud transceiver ASPs support sustained gross margin expansion

shifts.trend: Components $533M (+77%) + Systems $275M (+121%) FQ3; laser chips ramping at 200G lane density
shifts.note: Vertical integration into laser chips (Components) captures additional value vs transceiver assembly alone

shifts.trend: FQ3 guide $780-820M → printed $808M in range; FQ4 guide $850-900M; FY26 implied $2.9B vs $1.6B FY25
shifts.note: FY26 revenue near double FY25; margin expansion compounding — both levers working simultaneously

LITE.verdict: Lumentum is the most dramatic margin re-rating in the optics universe: from 28% gross margin (FY25 trough) to 44%+ in two quarters. Revenue near-doubling simultaneously. The structural driver: 800G cloud transceivers with 1.6T next. The risk is whether 1.6T products can sustain the 44-45% margin or whether hyperscaler pricing pressure compresses them.

MXL.headline: Inventory correction ended; infrastructure optical and wireless backhaul driving +43% YoY in Q1 2026 from -20% FY25 trough; Q2 guidance $145-160M continues recovery.

shifts.trend: FY25 $406M (-20% — inventory correction) → Q4 FY25 $95.9M (+12% QoQ) → Q1 2026 $137.2M (+43% YoY)
shifts.note: Channel inventory fully normalized; infrastructure optical (PAM4 DSP) and wireless backhaul recovering fastest

shifts.trend: Inventory days declining at distribution channel; Q1 2026 book-to-bill returning toward parity
shifts.note: 2024-2025 inventory correction was the buying opportunity; recovery now confirmed across two consecutive quarters

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM held ~51-53% through downcycle; Q1 2026 ~53%; Q2 guide ~54-55%
shifts.note: Gross margin maintained despite revenue -20% — demonstrates pricing power and mixed variable cost structure

shifts.trend: Top customer 13% (improved from historical 20%+); top 10 = 56%; customer base broadening
shifts.note: Infrastructure recovery broadening beyond lead customer — healthier revenue quality

MXL.verdict: MaxLinear is a mid-cycle recovery play: inventory correction fully behind, PAM4 DSP demand for 400G/800G AI datacenter spine and DCI recovering, wireless backhaul (5G) and DOCSIS 4.0 providing medium-term growth beyond optical. The -20% FY25 was the trough; +43% Q1 2026 is early-cycle re-acceleration. Q2 guide $145-160M confirms sustainability.
